quote: "In nature, nothing exists alone." — Rachel Carson
metaphor: A garden is like a team — each plant and animal has a job, and they all help each other grow!

systems_connection.big_idea: Living things are connected in ecosystems

systems_principles.description: Plants need animals and animals need plants — they depend on each other
systems_principles.grade1_manifestation: Bees need flowers for nectar, flowers need bees to spread pollen

systems_principles.description: Living things follow predictable patterns: grow, eat, respond
systems_principles.grade1_manifestation: All living things move toward food and water; plants grow toward light

systems_principles.description: Living things take in what they need and give out waste that others can use
systems_principles.grade1_manifestation: Plants take in water and sunlight, give off oxygen; animals breathe out carbon dioxide that plants need

systems_principles.description: Nature has repeating cycles that keep life going
systems_principles.grade1_manifestation: Water cycles from clouds to rain to soil to plants and back to clouds

discussion_prompts: What would happen if all the bees disappeared?
How does a garden help the animals that visit it?
What does a plant give to the world? What does it take?
systems_connection.future_connections: This foundation prepares students to understand food webs (Grade 3), ecosystems (Grade 5), and environmental stewardship (middle school)
